ops.title: codex/gpt-5.6-sol is blocked by OpenAI's cyber content filter on adversarial exploit-hunting security reviews — escalate those to fable
ops.body:
The Models table routes code/diff review, security review/audit, and verification of security-sensitive code to gpt-5.6-sol via codex. **But OpenAI's cybersecurity content filter blocks codex on adversarial, exploit-hunting security-review prompts** — anything framed as "find the bypass a hostile agent would run," RCE hunting, sandbox-escape, immutability-bypass, offensive vectors (`sitecustomize.py`/`.pth` shadowing, `setsid` escape, `core.fsmonitor`/`filter.*` RCE). Symptom: `codex exec` **exits 1 with an empty `-o` file** after doing real work; the run log ends with `ERROR: This content was flagged for possible cybersecurity risk … join the Trusted Access for Cyber program: https://chatgpt.com/cyber`. Hit this 3× in one session (an immutability lens + a judge/budget lens + a re-run), each time after ~80-100k tokens of actual analysis — the *final synthesis* is what gets filtered, not the reading.

**Why:** So a codex agent can silently return nothing. The `codex:codex-wrapper` compounds it — it backgrounds its `codex exec` and returns a stub ("kicked off in the background…") *before* codex finishes, so the empty/filtered result isn't even surfaced; check the reply file / `codex-run.log` directly.

**How to apply:** A content-filter block is a hard-miss → **escalate that specific review to fable** (per CLAUDE.md, hard sol miss escalates to fable; the hook that nudges "route security review to codex" doesn't know codex already refused). Fable has no trouble with clearly-authorized *defensive* review of the user's own repo — frame it "AUTHORIZED DEFENSIVE review of the user's own code; confirm the control is correctly implemented and find residual gaps," not "hunt for exploits." Straight *diff review* (judge/gate/lock/cost correctness) still passes the filter on codex — it's the offensive/RCE framing that trips it. When running codex directly, pipe to `-o` and always inspect the file + `codex-run.log` for the filter error rather than trusting a wrapper's early return. See [[spawnllm-claude-cli-gotchas]], [[experiment-at-home-shared-infra]].
